heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: The last storm was on 27 May 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 1 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.8.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (216°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 6.9°C), outside temperature (down 5.4°C), and inside temperature (down 1.7°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 8956 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 11986 lux) and cloud levels (up 559 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 296.2m lower than yesterday, 558.6m lower than the past week, 207.3m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1381m to a maximum of 5470m, averaging 2780m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 3340m and 2570m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; hot temperatures are forecast. Outside; the temperature will feel pleasantly warm. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and very little to no wind will be present. A few light raindrops may fall, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and strong and powerful sunlight. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
